Activity: Colin Bayer liked this
...
My team at Foot Locker is looking for two talented Sr. Data Analysts to join us. 

In these roles, you'll support the Foot Locker Data & Analytics team’s efforts in building foundational data products, providing advanced decision support and analysis to the entire retail operation, and aiding in the development of AI-powered tools & resources. 

This role will have exposure up and down the development cycle, from conceptualization to release and will work closely with developers, product owners, and leadership at every level. 

Please reach out if interested!

results.title: Hassan Chaudhry
highlights:
### Senior Data Scientist at [Foot Locker](https://www.linkedin.com/company/foot-locker)
...
Led multiple data science projects at Foot Locker and guided junior Data Scientists and Machine Learning Engineers. Additionally, I provided strategic input to senior leadership, worked closely with engineering teams to define architectures and collaborated across teams to improve processes and ensure value delivery via advanced analytics and data science. -- Demand Planning
...
- Developed advanced time-series models for demand prediction for the entire LSA market, including all gender, categories and Planning Groups providing smart start to the planners. Developed product embeddings (Visual, Text and Digital Impression) to find surrogate historic products to brand new products with no sales history in order to predict demand units. -- Built a what-if simulator tool to analyze the effects of changes in promotion price to overall sales for Levi’s brick and mortar business using causal impact model. -- E-commerce recommendation systems
...
- Implement and productionalize a unified embeddings approach for similar products search and out-of-stock recommendations, combining product attributes text embeddings, clickstream embeddings, and image embeddings. Recos are statsig. over control and live on the E-commerce platform -- Extract image embeddings from product catalog using a custom multi-task CNN; latent semantic analysis on product metadata to form product text embeddings; and word2vec on user clickstream behavior to form product co-click/co-purchase embeddings. -- Store Profiling
...
- Created a clustering algorithm to group current and future mainline stores with similar demand profiles for future merchandising and distribution decision making -- Customer lifetime value
...
- Develop and productionalize models to predict CLTV and propensity to purchase for millions of consumers, with a multi-stage approach, using 150+ handcrafted features, gradient boosting, and neural networks. Show less

results.title: T Negash
highlights:
Principal Data Scientist | Foot Locker | UTSA | PhD in Statistics
...
Principal Data Scientist at [Foot Locker](https://www.linkedin.com/company/foot-locker)
...
As a Principal Data Scientist at Foot Locker, I lead the development of advanced pricing machine learning models that drive dynamic pricing and optimization strategies. With over 3 years of experience in this role, I have successfully supported business operations across three major regions: North America, EMEA, and APAC. I have also partnered with business tech leads to implement reusable and robust solutions, leveraging my expertise in machine learning, data mining, and statistical programming. Prior to joining Foot Locker, I worked as a Senior Data Scientist at Mattress Firm, where I built forecasting, long-term causal estimation models using econometrics, machine learning, and predictive statistics. I also partnered with the Data Engineering team to build data infrastructure for supervised and unsupervised modeling techniques. Additionally, I developed business hypothesis statements and applied statistical testing to determine causality and generalize observations. I hold a PhD in Statistics from The University of Texas at San Antonio, and I am passionate about applying data science to solve complex business problems and drive business growth.
...

I spearhead the end-to-end development of advanced pricing machine learning models, driving dynamic pricing and pricing optimization strategies, as well as UAT and ongoing maintenance and enhancement of pricing application. highlights:
the merchandising process. Responsibilities The Data Integrity Specialists work with merchants on core global attribution for style creation Build item creation files based on core global attribution and other product attribution required to ensure all colors, style, divisional SKUs and UPC’s are created correctly Ensure all global style, color, divisional SKUs and UPC’s are created in a timely manner prior to agreed upon deadline times for purchase order writing Creation for all styles will occur either manually or through a pre-determined bulk upload process The Data Integrity Specialists will derive their work from the vendor deadline calendar to ensure that proper balance is maintained so that all work is submitted in a timely manner They will also be responsible for maintaining the vendor deadline calendar with accurate deadline dates The Data Integrity Specialists will be responsible for maintaining accurate attributions of all styles, colors and divisional SKUs This will be done through proper maintenance requests for updating, adding, and editing of potential errors as required The Data Integrity Specialists will work with the merchant teams to ensure that proper Family Codes are added in a timely manner to ensure that we are representing youth footwear on a unique product detail page This will aid in maximizing customer satisfaction and sales for our customers The Data Integrity Specialists are to work on weekly business reports such as the SCuL review and Power BI to determine what actions are necessary to ensure products flow online To accomplish this, they will work with the buying teams, marketing team, copywriting team and imaging team to ensure all work is completed The Data Integrity Specialists will be responsible for preparing sizing charts for all new vendors and updating existing sizing charts for current vendors as needed Skills Bachelor's degree from an accredited four-year college or university; or one to two years' related experience and/or training; or equivalent combination of education and experience Analytical - Synthesizes complex or diverse information; Collects and researches data; Uses intuition and experience to complement data; Designs workflows and procedures Ability to read, analyze, and interpret general business periodicals, professional journals, technical procedures, or governmental regulations Ability to write reports, business correspondence, and procedure manuals Ability to effectively present information and respond to questions from groups of managers, clients, customers, and the general public Ability to work with mathematical concepts such as probability and statistical inference, and fundamentals of plane and solid geometry and trigonometry Ability to apply concepts such as fractions, percentages, ratios, and proportions to practical situations Ability to solve practical problems and deal with a variety of concrete variables in situations where only limited standardization exists Ability to interpret a variety of instructions furnished in written, oral, diagram, or schedule form Knowledge of Excel Spreadsheet software and Microsoft Word Processing software The employee is regularly required to use hands to finger, handle, or feel and talk or hear The employee must regularly lift and/or move up to ten pounds Specific vision abilities required by this job include close vision and color vision Benefits Employee Discount Paid Time Off Medical | Dental | Vision Coverage 401(k) | Roth 401(k) Life Insurance Flexible Spending Account Opportunities for Advancement Tuition Reimbursement for Qualified Courses Strong Company Culture Employee Resource Groups Company Overview Foot Locker is a retailer of athletic footwear and apparel. results.title: lululemon Principal AI/ML Applied Scientist job with lululemon | 313841
highlights:
lululemon Principal AI/ML Applied Scientist job with lululemon | 313841
...
# lululemon Principal AI/ML Applied Scientist
...
Description & RequirementsWho we are lululemon is an innovative performance apparel company for yoga, running, training, and other athletic pursuits. Setting the bar in technical fabrics and functional design, we create transformational products and experiences that support people in moving, growing, connecting, and being well. We owe our success to our innovative product, emphasis on stores, commitment to our people, and the incredible connections we make in every community we're in. As a company, we focus on creating positive change to build a healthier, thriving future. In particular, that includes creating an equitable, inclusive and growth-focused environment for our people. About this team The Enterprise Data & AI organization is a strategic and operational driver of growth for lululemon, owning and building the data and AI platforms and services that enable the enterprise to operate with intelligence at scale. The team leads the design and delivery of a trusted unified data foundation, AI-driven data analytics and insights, and AI solutions across lululemon's vertically integrated retail ecosystem, while embedding strong data governance and responsible AI practices from the very beginning. By applying AI to critical business challenges and creating new, transformative AI solutions, the team helps reshape how lululemon operates. Through deep partnership with product, technology, and business teams, Enterprise Data & AI accelerates product innovation, unlocks measurable value, elevates guest and educator experiences, and drives enterprise efficiency. Core responsibilities As a Principal Applied AI/ML Scientist, you define and drive the solutions for enterprise ‑ level AI/ML use-cases, influencing multiple solutions across one or more business domains. You create business impact by extending the state of the art to invent new AI solutions. You operate as a top technical authority and thought leader, shaping how AI/ML becomes a durable competitive advantage across business lines. You lead the most complex, cross ‑ domain initiatives, establish retail industry ‑ leading methodologies, and influence long ‑ term solution approaches across the AI portfolio. You guide senior leaders on where and how to apply AI, ensuring solutions deliver sustained, measurable impact at scale. Examples of work include:
...
- Designing and inventing AI solutions across domains such as product design and innovation, planning and forecasting, agentic personalization and search, and multimodal content generation and enrichment
...
- Defining next ‑ generation models and architectures for generative AI, multimodal systems, and large ‑ scaleML-based optimization, leading to production
...
- Translating emerging AI research (e.g., LLMs, multimodal learning, foundation models) into enterprise ‑ ready capabilities with measurable business outcomes
...
- Bachelor's degree in computer science, data science, engineering, or related technical field, or equivalent experience; PhD preferred
...
- 11-15 years of AI/ML research experience in industry or equivalent academic experience
...
- Demonstrated ability to define enterprise ‑ level AI/ML solutions for previously unsolved problems, and influence executive decision ‑ making on technology direction and investment
...
- Proven track record of architecting and delivering large ‑ scale , complex AI/ML systems across multiple domains with significant organizational impact
...
- Deep expertise in advanced AI/ML approaches, including areas such as generative AI, NLP, computer vision, multimodal learning, or time ‑ series modeling, and ability to translate research into production systems
...
- Expert ‑ level experience with modern ML tools and ecosystems (e.g., Python, PyTorch , cloud ML platforms, distributed training/inference systems, and scalable data pipelines)

## Job description
...
Google's software engineers develop the next-generation technologies that change how billions of users connect, explore, and interact with information and one another. Our products need to handle information at massive scale, and extend well beyond web search. We're looking for engineers who bring fresh ideas from all areas, including information retrieval, distributed computing, large-scale system design, networking and data storage, security, artificial intelligence, natural language processing, UI design and mobile; the list goes on and is growing every day. As a software engineer, you will work on a specific project critical to Google’s needs with opportunities to switch teams and projects as you and our fast-paced business grow and evolve. We need our engineers to be versatile, display leadership qualities and be enthusiastic to take on new problems across the full-stack as we continue to push technology forward.
...
As a Software Engineer on the Shopping Ads team, you will be an "AI First" engineer dedicated to solving complex issues using Generative AI . You will design, implement, and launch innovative Large Language Model (LLM) and Machine Learning (ML) systems to enhance the shopping journey for users worldwide.

This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for an AI / ML Engineer in the United States.This role sits at the intersection of machine learning engineering, AI systems design, and large-scale data-driven decision-making, focused on solving complex challenges in the energy and utility sector. You will contribute to the development of advanced AI and ML solutions that combine predictive modeling, optimization, and generative AI to support critical infrastructure and sustainability goals. Working closely with data scientists and software engineers, you will help design, build, and deploy scalable machine learning systems that power real-world applications. The environment is highly collaborative and innovation-driven, with a strong emphasis on applied AI, production readiness, and measurable impact. You will work across diverse use cases including forecasting, geospatial analysis, and intelligent decision systems. This is a high-impact role where your work directly supports the modernization and efficiency of energy systems.Accountabilities

...

...
- 7+ years of experience building, deploying, and maintaining machine learning systems in production environments.
...
- Strong proficiency in Python, including experience building reusable packages and scalable data pipelines.
...
- Hands-on experience with cloud platforms such as AWS, Azure, or GCP, including CI/CD and containerized deployments.
...
- Solid understanding of ML system design, MLOps principles, and end-to-end model lifecycle management.
...
- Experience with distributed computing frameworks and relational/non-relational databases.
...
- Familiarity with Databricks and large-scale data processing environments.
...
- Experience working with generative AI frameworks such as LangChain, LangGraph, LlamaIndex, Hugging Face, or AutoGen.
...
- Exposure to agentic AI systems, AI orchestration, or LLM-based decision workflows is highly desirable.

We’re building the next generation of football intelligence. FAIR is an AI that goes beyond traditional stats to analyse every action, movement, and decision on the pitch, and evaluate how they impact team performance. Trained to understand how football should be played, FAIR gives users access to advanced football intelligence that helps them make better decisions, whether they are fans, fantasy football players, analysts, bettors or strategists.
...
As an AI Engineer Intern, you will work directly on the development of FAIR Chat and other AI-powered products. You will help design, build, and optimize LLM-powered applications, agent workflows, retrieval systems, and backend services. This role is ideal for a student or recent graduate who is passionate about AI, software engineering, and football. Internship is fully remote, for a duration of 3 to 12 months, and may be part-time or full-time (preferred).
...

- Develop backend APIs and services using Python and FastAPI
- Work with football datasets and football intelligence models
- Collaborate on cloud deployment and infrastructure across Azure and Google Cloud Platform
- Contribute to backend/frontend features using ReactJS and NodeJS
- Implement monitoring, evaluation, and testing frameworks for AI systems
- Participate in code reviews and software development best practices
